title: rosa pendulina subvar. nuriae
intro: Rosa pendulina subvar. nuriae, also known as the Alpine Rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa pendulina species, a fragrant and flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Traditionally, the medicinal properties of the Alpine Rose have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of the Alpine Rose include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of the Alpine Rose include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
